feedback: Clean execution. The payoff — 'I don't write less code. I write less bad code.' — is a genuine insight with a satisfying inversion. Fragments land naturally ('2,400 lines of python.', 'mostly deleting.'). Rhythm is punchy without being broetry. 293 chars with URL. No kill switch violations.

dimension_notes.voice: pass — multiple fragments, 4+ first-person refs, strong sentence-length variation, no banned words or phrases, zero hedges, contractions throughout
dimension_notes.framing: pass — reacts to one specific experience, opens with a micro-take ('shipped something solo last week'), feels like a person not a brand
dimension_notes.platform: pass — 293 chars total including URL, single post, lowercase, technically specific (2,400 lines, three days vs two weeks), casual and community-toned
dimension_notes.epistemic: pass — confident first-person experience claim, appropriate for the type; no speculative claims requiring uncertainty markers
dimension_notes.structure: pass — excellent burstiness (3-word fragments next to 8-word sentences), no rhythmic monotony, stops cleanly on the insight
dimension_notes.framework: pass — Orwell clarity throughout, fact before take (numbers → timeline → surprise → insight), every claim grounded in personal experience
